source: Deep Supervised Hashing for Fast Image Retrieval .pdf

concepts.finding: Deep Supervised Hashing (DSH) jointly learns image representations and hash functions in an end-to-end manner, achieving compact binary codes preserving semantic similarity from labeled data.
concepts.implication: End-to-end learning of binary hash codes produces more compact and accurate representations, applicable to learning efficient query-document matching codes.
concepts.open_question: Can end-to-end learned hash codes for query-document matching match the accuracy of full-precision embeddings at 1/10th storage cost?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: End-to-end learned 64-bit hash codes for query-document matching will achieve within 3% recall of 768-dim full-precision BERT embeddings while using 96x less storage.
